title: Proclamation by the President Imposing a Temporary Import Surcharge to Address Fundamental International Payments Problems
summary: This proclamation, issued by the President of the United States, asserts the authority to impose a temporary surcharge on imports with the aim of addressing fundamental international payments problems. It cites constitutional authority and statutory authority under sections 122, 301, and 604 of title 3, United States Code. The document outlines the purpose of the surcharge, the scope of imports affected, the anticipated duration, any exemptions, and the administrative and enforcement mechanisms for collection and compliance, as well as the provisions for potential adjustment or termination of the surcharge as described within the proclamation.

title: Proclamation: Continuing the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries
summary: This presidential action, issued under authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, continues the policy of suspending duty-free de minimis treatment for goods from all countries entering the United States, thereby maintaining the current approach to customs treatment for these shipments.

title: Executive Action Ending Certain Tariff Actions
summary: The President, citing the Constitution and authorities including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, as amended (19 U.S.C. 2483), announces the termination of certain tariff actions that had been in effect. The action identifies the scope of the tariff measures being ended and describes the administrative steps required to implement the termination, including any agency actions and regulatory adjustments. It specifies the intended effective date and any transitional provisions governing import obligations and compliance as the tariff actions are rescinded.

title: Executive Order Promoting National Defense by Ensuring Adequate Supply of Elemental Phosphorus and Glyphosate-Based Herbicides
summary: This executive order, issued under the authority of the Defense Production Act of 1950 and section 301 of title 3 of the United States Code, directs federal agencies to assess and strengthen the domestic supply chains for elemental phosphorus and glyphosate-based herbicides. It identifies supply-chain vulnerabilities, calls for interagency coordination with the private sector to mitigate risks, and authorizes appropriate DPA actions to prioritize production, allocate resources, and support expanded domestic manufacturing and distribution. The order outlines responsibilities for implementing measures to prevent shortages and requires periodic reporting on progress and outcomes to the President.

title: Presidential Proclamation - President George Washington's Birthday, 2026
summary: This Presidential Proclamation designates February 22, 2026 as President George Washington's Birthday and marks the 250th anniversary of American independence in 2026. It honors George Washington's roles as a leader in the American Revolution and as a foundational figure in the United States, highlighting his contributions to liberty and the nation's founding principles. The proclamation invites federal agencies and the public to observe the day with appropriate ceremonies and educational activities that reflect on Washington's enduring impact on American democracy and civic life.

title: Adjusting Tariff Duties in Response to Threats Posed by the Government of the Russian Federation
summary: This presidential action uses authorities granted by the Constitution and federal law, including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 604 of the Trade Act of 1974, to modify duties on imports from the Government of the Russian Federation. The objective is to respond to threats posed by the Russian government and to address national security and foreign policy considerations. The action may alter existing tariff schedules, exemptions, or applicable duties for goods of Russian origin and outlines steps for implementing and enforcing these changes. It reflects the use of trade and economic powers to address national security concerns. The measures are designed to be implemented in a manner consistent with applicable law and regulatory procedures.

title: Executive Order Establishing a United States Arms Transfer Strategy
summary: This presidential executive order establishes U.S. policy for arms transfers with the objective of prioritizing the use of American-manufactured military equipment and leveraging domestic manufacturing capacity to influence international defense exports. The order notes that domestic production provides a strategic advantage and directs federal agencies to coordinate and implement policies that align procurement, licensing, and export-control activities with the goal of expanding defense exports while maintaining national security and compliance with applicable laws. The document begins with a stated purpose and sets forth the general approach for implementing the strategy across relevant agencies.
